content.text: Administrators can manage templates for restricted commands that are not allowed on servers and can directly grant or revoke access permissions to servers. Command restriction is string-based, and support for regular expressions is also available.
Create Template`. Enter the following details:
Enter a name to identify the template.
Enter additional information about the template.
STEP 2 Select whether to allow or deny commands registered in the command template.
Configure the commands to be denied. Any commands other than the configured ones will be allowed.
Configure the commands to be allowed. Only the configured commands will be permitted, and all others will be denied.
Configure the command policy for commands executed through the web terminal on the server.
Commands can be entered as keywords.
Commands can be entered as regular expressions.
Configure the functionality policy for commands executed via web SFTP or FTP on the server.
button at the bottom right to finalize the configuration.
menu and select the user or group to whom you want to grant the privilege.
STEP 3 Choose the server group from the left-hand list for which you want to grant access permissions.
STEP 4 The associated servers and accounts within that group will appear on the right-hand side. Select the server and accounts you wish to grant permissions to.
On the right-hand side, select the
you want to grant permissions for.
At the bottom right, select the
that can access the selected server(s).
STEP 6 Set the following policy, then click the
button to finalize the permission assignment.
Set an expiration date for the access permission (default is 1 year, max is 1 year).
Specify the protocol(s) used for server access.
Set restricted command sets for server access. Click Command Template Details to review the specific restrictions.
Set the start time for server access.
Set the end time for server access.
Choose the days of the week when access is allowed.
Define the IP addresses from which access is allowed.
Determine whether the commands used in the connected session should be logged through this permission.
Determine whether the detection of forbidden commands within scripts and aliases should be enabled when called.
Set whether the QueryPie Agent allows server access under this Permission.
Limit the number of concurrent sessions a user can have on a server.
Set the duration (in minutes) after which an inactive session will be terminated.
